skill.name: github-release-assistant
skill.description: Generate bilingual GitHub release README files from repository metadata, guided configuration, and existing documentation.
skill.summary: Create English and Chinese GitHub release READMEs from local project metadata.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk crypto, reconnaissance, and shell execution patterns, but review found these are mostly Markdown text, regex literals, and fenced-code formatting. The skill does include a Python generator that reads repository files and can overwrite README files, plus documented git commit and push guidance that requires explicit user confirmation. No malicious intent, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: README overwrite capability
medium_findings.description: The generator writes README.md and README.zh.md in the selected output directory, and --overwrite permits replacement of existing files. This is legitimate for release documentation, but users should review diffs before committing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write paths and overwrite flag are explicit in the script. The behavior is purposeful documentation generation, not malicious file tampering.

medium_findings.title: Optional git publishing workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill can guide git status, diff, add, commit, and push actions after user confirmation. This can publish generated files to a remote repository if the user approves.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The git workflow is documented plainly and requires confirmation. The risk is operational rather than hidden command execution.

medium_findings.title: Local filesystem metadata collection
medium_findings.description: The script reads README, config, project structure, license, requirements, and docs-derived metadata from the target project. This is expected for README generation but should be limited to intended repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed repository files are read directly to build README content. No evidence shows secret harvesting or transmission.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are generated README links
low_findings.description: The flagged URLs are shields.io badge image links, a python.org reference, and localhost quick-start text. They do not create runtime network requests from the script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are embedded into Markdown output strings. There is no HTTP client, fetch call, or upload path in the reviewed code.

low_findings.title: Static crypto and reconnaissance matches are text false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak-crypto and reconnaissance locations are words in descriptions, reference prose, CJK regex handling, or argparse help text. No cryptographic algorithm or reconnaissance command is implemented there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines contain documentation text, variable names, regex character ranges, or CLI descriptions. None performs cryptography or system probing.

low_findings.title: Backtick execution matches are Markdown and string formatting
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks in SKILL.md and the script are Markdown command examples or generated fenced code blocks. The Python script does not call subprocess, shell, eval, or os.system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are Markdown inline code, regex for fenced code blocks, or strings that render Markdown fences. No executable shell sink is present in those lines.

low_findings.title: Absolute local skill path in quick start
low_findings.description: The quick-start command uses a specific /Users path into a .codex skill directory. This is not malicious, but it is non-portable and exposes an author-local path pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The absolute path is visible and references a hidden .codex directory. It is a portability and hygiene issue, not a confirmed compromise vector.

dangerous_patterns.title: Controlled file overwrite
dangerous_patterns.description: The script supports --overwrite and writes README outputs into a caller-selected output directory. Users should run it only on repositories they intend to modify.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The overwrite behavior is explicit and bounded to README generation. It is risky if used carelessly but not hidden or malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository publish commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to ask before git add, commit, and push. This workflow can change remote repository state when the user confirms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are workflow instructions and include an approval step. The remote publication risk remains real after approval.

content.value_statement: Release documentation is slow when teams need English and Chinese READMEs. This skill generates structured bilingual READMEs from repository metadata and guided config.
actual_capabilities: Generates README.md and README.zh.md from local repository metadata.
Uses optional release_assistant.json fields for names, taglines, features, quick start steps, license, and credits.
Extracts content from README.md, PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, config files, requirements files, and app.py port settings.
Creates concise English and Chinese sections for overview, contents, capabilities, components, quick start, configuration, structure, license, and credits.
Supports English-only, Chinese-only, or bilingual output through a command-line language option.
Guides review, staging, commit, and push steps when the user explicitly requests release preparation.
limitations: It does not publish GitHub releases through the GitHub API.
Generated prose depends on available metadata and may need human editing.
The quick-start command in SKILL.md contains an author-local absolute path.
Using --overwrite can replace existing README files in the target output directory.

use_cases.title: Prepare open-source release docs
use_cases.description: Create bilingual README files before tagging a public release.

use_cases.title: Standardize project handoff documentation
use_cases.description: Generate consistent project summaries, capabilities, setup steps, and structure notes for internal repositories.

use_cases.title: Localize developer onboarding material
use_cases.description: Produce matching English and Chinese README drafts from one repository context.

prompt_templates.title: Generate basic READMEs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for default bilingual output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate English and Chinese README files for this repository. Review the current files first and ask me before overwriting anything.

prompt_templates.title: Use release config
prompt_templates.scenario: Use structured project details from a config file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use release_assistant.json to generate bilingual GitHub README files. Keep the sections concise and preserve factual project details.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh release documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Update existing documentation for a release candidate.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refresh README.md and README.zh.md for this release. Compare the diff after generation and point out content that needs my review.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare commit workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with review and optional git operations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate bilingual release READMEs, show me the diff, propose a commit message, and wait for my approval before staging or pushing changes.

output_examples.input: Generate release READMEs for a Python application with existing README and requirements files.
output_examples.output: A pair of English and Chinese README drafts with project overview, capabilities, setup steps, detected configuration files, license, and language links.

output_examples.input: Prepare this repository for a bilingual documentation release.
output_examples.output: Updated README files plus a review checklist covering changed sections, missing details, and a suggested documentation commit message.

output_examples.input: Use my release_assistant.json values for the next release.
output_examples.output: README content aligned with the supplied project names, taglines, features, components, quick-start steps, license, and credits.

best_practices: Review generated diffs before accepting README overwrites.
Fill release_assistant.json when project metadata is missing or ambiguous.
Confirm git add, commit, and push actions separately.
anti_patterns: Do not run --overwrite without checking existing README content first.
Do not use generated release text as a substitute for technical review.
Do not push documentation changes before verifying both language versions.

faq.question: Does this skill create GitHub releases?
faq.answer: No. It generates README documentation and can guide git workflow steps after user confirmation.

faq.question: Can it generate both English and Chinese documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The script can generate README.md, README.zh.md, or both files.

faq.question: What files does it read?
faq.answer: It reads local project metadata such as README.md, config.json, PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, requirements files, LICENSE, and app.py when present.

faq.question: Will it overwrite existing README files?
faq.answer: Only when the overwrite option is used. Users should inspect diffs before committing changes.

faq.question: Does it send project data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network upload was found. The hardcoded URLs are Markdown badge and localhost links.

faq.question: Is the included quick-start command portable?
faq.answer: Not fully. SKILL.md shows an author-local absolute path that users may need to replace.
